# Fragment previews are not regenerated by corpus re-ingest — automate ASAP

**Date:** 2026-07-05 (Francisco: "document this for solving ASAP")
**Status:** **Resolved (2026-07-07)** — all four fix items below implemented:
`enqueue_preview_regeneration_for_movement` exists in `services/fragments.py`
and the ingest path calls it per accepted movement after commit (unit-tested
in `test_fragment_service.py` / `test_ingestion_service.py`). Dispatch now
runs **in-process by default** (ADR-034), which also retires the broker-cost
caveat at the bottom — no worker window or Upstash traffic is needed for the
regeneration. The script stays for ad-hoc recovery; the runbook step is now a
verification.
**Severity:** Medium (silent staleness of user-visible previews after any re-ingest)

## The gap

Re-ingesting a movement replaces its normalized MEI, which invalidates the
cached SVG previews of every fragment on that movement. The bulk upload path
(`services/ingestion.py`) re-dispatches `generate_incipit` and
`ingest_movement_analysis` per movement, but **not**
`render_fragment_preview` — previews regenerate only on a per-fragment
submit / bar-range edit (`services/fragments.py`).

ADR-008 already specifies the fix:
`FragmentService.enqueue_preview_regeneration_for_movement(movement_id)` —
"the correction workflow must call [it]… which enqueues the
`render_fragment_preview` Celery task for every fragment on that movement
whose status is `submitted` or above." **This entry point was never
implemented**, and nothing in the upload path calls anything equivalent.

## Current workaround (manual, easy to forget)

`scripts/regenerate_fragment_previews.py` (run after
`verify_reingest_mc_stability.py` is clean), per
`step-9-reingestion-runbook.md` §5. It was applied on the 2026-07-05 local
54-movement re-ingest (11 submitted/approved fragments re-rendered, 0
errors) and must be applied on every staging/production re-ingest by hand.

The failure mode when forgotten is **silent**: previews keep rendering from
the pre-re-ingest MEI (e.g. the pre-ADR-031-amendment double clefs), with no
warning anywhere, until someone notices visually.

## Fix to implement

1. Implement `FragmentService.enqueue_preview_regeneration_for_movement`
   per ADR-008 (fragments with status `submitted`/`approved` on the
   movement; enqueue after commit, mirroring the submit-path pattern).
2. Call it from the ingest service for every **upserted-over-existing**
   movement (a movement ingested for the first time has no fragments — the
   call is a no-op there, so calling unconditionally is also fine).
3. Keep the script for ad-hoc recovery; the runbook step then becomes a
   verification ("confirm the tasks were enqueued") rather than an action.
4. Unit-test the wiring: re-ingesting a movement with a submitted fragment
   enqueues exactly its preview task.

Watch the broker cost on staging: N fragments = N tasks inside the ingest
window (Upstash is metered), which is exactly when the worker is already on —
no extra window needed.
